overview.value: Ladyfinger Cactus forms clusters of narrow cylindrical stems covered in small spines. It is compact, drought tolerant, and ideal for bright windowsills.

careDifficultyDescription.value: Very low care if kept dry and bright.

light.value: Bright direct or very bright indirect light.

water.value: Water sparingly only when soil is bone dry.

humidityDescription.value: Prefers dry air and excellent airflow.

diseaseRiskDescription.value: Rot is the main risk when soil stays wet.

fertilizer.value: Feed once in spring or early summer with cactus fertilizer.

tips.value: Use a gritty mineral-heavy mix and never let the pot sit in water.
